title: Generate a QR Code Inside Word
description: Create a scannable QR code for any URL directly inside a Word document — no plugins, no internet, completely offline.
steps: Click where you want the QR code
Press Ctrl + F9 to create a field code bracket { }
Inside the brackets, type: displaybarcode "https://yoururl.com" qr \3
Press F9 to render it into a real QR code
result: A scannable QR code appears inline in your document. Right-click → Format Object to resize it.

title: Open the Emoji Picker Anywhere
description: Access every emoji, symbol, kaomoji, and special character instantly from any text field on Windows.
steps: Click inside any text input or document
Press Windows key + . (period) or Windows key + ; (semicolon)
result: A floating emoji picker opens. Search by name, browse by category, or pick from recently used ones.

title: Reopen a Tab You Just Closed
description: Instantly bring back a tab you accidentally closed — works in Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari.
steps: Press Ctrl + Shift + T (Windows/Linux) or Cmd + Shift + T (Mac)
result: The last closed tab reopens exactly where you left it. Press again to keep restoring more closed tabs.

title: Paste Text Without Formatting
description: Copy text from a styled webpage and paste it as plain text — no fonts, colors, or sizes carried over.
steps: Copy text as usual (Ctrl + C)
Instead of Ctrl + V, press Ctrl + Shift + V
result: The text pastes without any formatting. In apps that don't support this shortcut, paste into Notepad first, then copy again.

title: Clipboard History — See Everything You've Copied
description: Windows keeps a history of everything you've copied. Stop losing the thing you copied two steps ago.
steps: First enable it: Settings → System → Clipboard → Turn on Clipboard History
Press Windows key + V to open the clipboard history panel
Click any item to paste it
result: A panel shows your last 25 copied items — text, images, and more. You can also pin frequently used items.

title: Insert Today's Date Instantly in Excel
description: Stamp the current date into any cell in one keystroke — no typing, no formatting required.
steps: Click any cell in Excel
Press Ctrl + ; (semicolon) to insert today's date
Press Ctrl + Shift + ; to insert the current time
result: Today's date appears instantly in the cell. Note: this is a static value — it won't change tomorrow like the TODAY() formula.

title: Use Google Translate as a Proxy Reader
description: Access paywalled or region-blocked articles by routing them through Google Translate — completely free.
steps: Go to translate.google.com
In the source box, paste the full URL of the blocked article
Set target language to English (or any language)
Click the translated link that appears
result: Google fetches and renders the page through its servers, bypassing most soft paywalls and regional blocks.

title: Take a Screenshot of a Full Webpage
description: Capture an entire webpage as a PNG — including parts below the fold — without any extension.
steps: Open the page you want to capture
Press F12 to open DevTools
Press Ctrl + Shift + P to open the command palette
Type 'screenshot' and select 'Capture full size screenshot'
result: A full-page PNG downloads automatically — the entire scrollable page in one image.

title: Find Any File Modified Today on Windows
description: Instantly filter your file explorer to show only files changed or created today — great for finding that file you saved somewhere.
steps: Open File Explorer and navigate to a folder
Click the Search bar at the top
Type: datemodified:today
Press Enter
result: Only files modified today appear. You can also use datemodified:this week or datemodified:yesterday.

title: Pin a Tab to Save Space in Chrome
description: Shrink frequently-used tabs like Gmail or Spotify to tiny icons so they never accidentally get closed.
steps: Right-click on any tab
Select 'Pin tab'
result: The tab shrinks to just the favicon and moves to the left. Pinned tabs can't be accidentally closed and persist across restarts.

title: Turn Any YouTube Video into a GIF
description: Create an instant shareable GIF from any moment in a YouTube video — directly in the browser URL bar.
steps: Open any YouTube video
In the URL bar, add 'gif' before 'youtube' — e.g., www.gifyoutube.com/watch?v=...
Select your start time and duration on the tool that opens
Download or share the GIF
result: A free GIF generator opens with the exact video. Clip any segment up to 10 seconds into a shareable GIF.

title: Use Your Phone as a Webcam on Windows
description: Windows 11 has a built-in Phone Link feature that lets you use your Android phone's camera as a PC webcam — no apps needed.
steps: Open Settings → Bluetooth & devices → Mobile devices
Enable 'Allow this PC to access your mobile devices'
Link your Android phone via the Phone Link app
In any video call app, select 'Virtual Camera' as the webcam source
result: Your phone's high-quality rear camera works as a webcam. Far better quality than most built-in laptop cameras.

title: Snipping Tool Anywhere, Instantly
description: Take a precise screenshot of any area on your screen in one shortcut — no apps needed, no PrintScreen hassle.
steps: Press Windows + Shift + S
Drag to select any area of the screen
The screenshot is copied to clipboard immediately
result: Paste it directly into any chat, email, or document with Ctrl + V. A notification also lets you annotate it before saving.

title: Snap Windows to Any Side with the Keyboard
description: Arrange two or more windows side by side without touching the mouse — perfect for dual-screen productivity.
steps: Focus any window
Press Windows + Left Arrow to snap it to the left half
Press Windows + Right Arrow to snap to the right
Press Windows + Up/Down to maximize or minimize
result: Windows suggests filling the other half with another open app. Press Windows + Z for the full Snap Layouts grid on Windows 11.

title: Open Task Manager Directly
description: Most people press Ctrl+Alt+Delete and then click Task Manager. There's a faster way.
steps: Press Ctrl + Shift + Esc
result: Task Manager opens instantly — no intermediate screen. Right-click the taskbar also has 'Task Manager' for mouse users.

title: God Mode — Windows Control Panel Superpower
description: One hidden folder gives you access to every single Windows setting in one place — no digging through menus.
steps: Right-click the desktop → New → Folder
Name the folder exactly: GodMode.{ED7BA470-8E54-465E-825C-99712043E01C}
Press Enter — the folder icon changes to a control panel icon
Open it to see 200+ settings organized by category
result: Every Windows setting, system tool, and configuration option in one scrollable list. Works on Windows 10 and 11.

title: Google Search Like a Pro — Power Operators
description: Most people search with plain words. These operators let you find exactly what you want in seconds.
steps: site:reddit.com your topic — search only within Reddit
filetype:pdf machine learning — find PDF files on a topic
"exact phrase" — search for exact word order
-word — exclude a word from results
before:2020 after:2019 — filter by date range
result: Combine operators: site:reddit.com "productivity tips" -ads gives you Reddit threads about productivity with no sponsored results.

title: Google as a Calculator, Timer and Unit Converter
description: Google Search has a built-in calculator, unit converter, and timer — no need to open any other app.
steps: Type 'timer 25 minutes' to start a countdown timer
Type '500 USD to INR' for live currency conversion
Type '100 km to miles' for unit conversion
Type 'sqrt(144)' or '15% of 340' for math
Type 'flip a coin' or 'roll a dice' for randomness
result: Everything runs directly in the search results page — the timer even rings in your browser.

title: Excel Flash Fill — Auto-Complete Any Pattern
description: Excel watches what you're typing and automatically fills the rest of a column following the same pattern — no formulas needed.
steps: Type the first example of what you want (e.g., extract first name from 'John Smith')
Type the second example manually
Press Ctrl + E
Excel fills the entire column following your pattern
result: Works for splitting names, reformatting phone numbers, extracting emails, changing date formats — any consistent text pattern.

title: Show All Formulas in an Excel Sheet
description: See every formula in every cell at once — perfect for auditing a spreadsheet someone else built.
steps: Press Ctrl + ` (grave accent — the key to the left of 1)
All formulas show instead of their results
Press Ctrl + ` again to toggle back to values
result: The entire sheet switches to formula view. Columns auto-widen to show full formula text. Great for debugging.

title: Lock a Cell Reference with F4
description: When dragging formulas down a column, cell references shift automatically. F4 locks them so they stay fixed.
steps: Click inside a formula on a cell reference (e.g., A1)
Press F4 to toggle between: A1 → $A$1 → A$1 → $A1 → A1
$A$1 = fully locked | A$1 = row locked | $A1 = column locked
result: Use $A$1 to lock a reference completely so it doesn't shift when you drag the formula to other cells.

title: Multi-Cursor Editing in VS Code
description: Edit multiple lines simultaneously without find & replace — one of the most powerful productivity tricks in any code editor.
steps: Hold Alt and click anywhere to add extra cursors
Or: Select a word, then press Ctrl + D to select the next occurrence
Keep pressing Ctrl + D to add more occurrences
Type to edit all selected occurrences at once
result: All cursors type simultaneously. Press Escape to return to single cursor. Works in VS Code, Cursor, and most modern editors.

title: VS Code Command Palette — Do Anything
description: The Command Palette is a searchable menu of every VS Code command — faster than any menu.
steps: Press Ctrl + Shift + P (or Cmd + Shift + P on Mac)
Type any command name — 'format', 'git commit', 'theme'
Press Enter to run it
result: You can format code, change color theme, toggle terminal, run tasks, and install extensions — all without a mouse.

title: Middle-Click to Open Links in Background
description: Stop right-clicking → 'Open in new tab'. Middle-click is faster and opens links in the background so you keep reading.
steps: Hover over any link
Click your scroll wheel (middle button) instead of left-clicking
result: The link opens in a new tab in the background — your current page stays focused. Middle-click a tab to close it too.

title: Search Within Any Website from Chrome's Address Bar
description: Search YouTube, Amazon, or any site directly from the Chrome address bar without going to the site first.
steps: Type the website name in the address bar (e.g. 'youtube.com')
Press Tab — the bar changes to 'Search YouTube'
Type your search term and press Enter
result: You're taken directly to the search results on that site. Works automatically for most sites after you've visited them once.

title: View Source Code of Any Webpage
description: See the full HTML of any webpage — useful for learning how sites are built or finding hidden content.
steps: Go to any webpage
Press Ctrl + U (Windows) or Cmd + Option + U (Mac)
Or type 'view-source:' before the URL in the address bar
result: The full HTML source opens in a new tab. Use Ctrl + F to search for specific elements, text, or metadata.

title: Gmail + Trick — Infinite Email Addresses
description: You have infinite unique email addresses with your single Gmail account — great for filtering or bypassing 'one account per person' limits.
steps: Your Gmail is: yourname@gmail.com
Add +anything after your name: yourname+netflix@gmail.com
Emails sent to any +tag version arrive in your inbox
Set up a Gmail filter on 'To: yourname+netflix@gmail.com' to auto-label them
result: Signup to every service with a different +tag. See exactly who sells your email by which tag gets spam.

title: Google Maps — Save Offline Map of Any City
description: Download a city map so it works fully without internet — navigation, search, and directions all offline.
steps: Open Google Maps on your phone
Search for a city or area
Tap the city name at the bottom to open its info card
Tap the three-dot menu → Download offline map
Adjust the region and tap Download
result: The map works without any internet connection. Perfect for travel, areas with poor signal, or saving mobile data.

title: Undo Anything in Windows File Explorer
description: Accidentally deleted, renamed, or moved a file? Ctrl + Z works in File Explorer just like in Word.
steps: Open File Explorer
Press Ctrl + Z immediately after the mistake
Press multiple times to undo multiple file operations
result: Files are restored, renames are reversed, moves are undone. Works for rename, move, copy, and delete operations.

title: Chrome DevTools — Pick Any Color from the Screen
description: Chrome has a built-in eyedropper that can pick any color from anywhere on your screen — not just the webpage.
steps: Press F12 to open DevTools
In the Elements tab, click any color swatch in the CSS panel
The color picker opens — click the eyedropper icon (bottom left)
Click anywhere on your screen to pick that color
result: The exact hex/RGB/HSL code appears. Copy it directly. Works across any visible pixel on your entire screen.

title: Drag Any Tab into Its Own Window
description: Split your browser tabs into separate windows instantly — useful for side-by-side comparison or dual monitor setups.
steps: Click and hold any tab
Drag it away from the tab bar
Release — it becomes its own window
Drag it back to merge it into another window
result: The tab opens as a standalone window. Drag multiple tabs together to merge them. Great for putting a YouTube video on one monitor.

title: Read Any Wikipedia Article Offline with a URL Trick
description: Load a clean, printable version of any Wikipedia article — faster, no ads, no sidebars.
steps: Open any Wikipedia article
In the URL, change 'en.wikipedia.org/wiki/' to 'en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/'
Or add ?action=render at the end of the URL
result: A clean article-only view loads — perfect for reading. The mobile version also loads significantly faster on slow connections.

title: PowerPoint: Laser Pointer + Drawing During Presentation
description: Turn your cursor into a laser pointer or annotation pen while presenting — no extra hardware needed.
steps: Start your slideshow (press F5)
Hold Ctrl and press the left mouse button — cursor becomes a laser pointer
Press Ctrl + P to switch to pen mode for drawing on slides
Press E to erase all drawings
Press Ctrl + A to switch back to arrow
result: Highlight points live during your presentation. The audience sees the laser or annotations on the projected screen.

title: Google Docs — Type with Your Voice
description: Dictate entire documents hands-free in Google Docs — supports punctuation commands and works in 60+ languages.
steps: Open Google Docs in Chrome
Go to Tools → Voice typing
Click the microphone icon that appears
Speak naturally — say 'period', 'comma', 'new line' for punctuation
result: Your speech is transcribed in real-time with surprising accuracy. Say 'select all', 'bold', 'italics' for formatting commands.

title: Find the Exact Font Used on Any Website
description: Identify any font on any website in seconds — no extensions needed.
steps: Right-click any text on a webpage
Select 'Inspect'
In the Elements panel, click the 'Computed' tab
Scroll to find 'font-family' — this is the exact font name
result: You see the full font stack. Search the font name on Google Fonts or MyFonts to find and download it.

title: Google Search: Find Flights and Hotels Directly
description: Google has a built-in flight and hotel search that's often cheaper and faster than dedicated booking sites.
steps: Search: 'flights from Chennai to Dubai'
A full flight finder appears — filter by date, stops, airline
Search: 'hotels in Paris under 100 dollars' for hotel search
Click 'Explore' on the flight results to see a price calendar
result: Google shows prices across all airlines with a price calendar showing cheapest days to fly. Links go directly to booking sites.

title: Windows: Create an Invisible Folder
description: Make a folder with no name and no visible icon — hiding it in plain sight on your desktop.
steps: Create a new folder on the desktop
Rename it: hold Alt, type 0160 on the numpad, press Enter (this is a non-breaking space — an invisible character)
Right-click → Properties → Customize → Change Icon
Select a blank/transparent icon from the list
result: The folder appears invisible on the desktop. It's still there — click the area where it is to open it.

title: YouTube: Loop Any Section of a Video
description: Loop a specific 10-second clip of a YouTube video — not the whole video. Great for learning dance moves, solos, or tutorials.
steps: Go to the YouTube video
Right-click on the video player
Select 'Loop' to loop the entire video
For a specific section: open the video on youtu.be (or add it yourself at youtuberepeater.com)
result: The right-click Loop option endlessly repeats the full video. Third-party tools let you set AB loop points for specific sections.

title: Recover Unsaved Word or Excel Files
description: Closed without saving? Office autosaves recovery files every few minutes — here's how to find them.
steps: In Word or Excel: File → Info → Manage Document
Click 'Recover Unsaved Documents' (Word) or 'Recover Unsaved Workbooks' (Excel)
Browse the recovery folder that opens
Open your file and immediately Save As to a proper location
result: Office keeps autosave drafts for 4 days. You may lose the last few minutes of work, but rarely everything.

title: Zoom In on Any Screen Without an App
description: Windows has a built-in magnifier — zoom into any part of your screen for accessibility or detailed viewing.
steps: Press Windows + Plus (+) to open the Magnifier and zoom in
Press Windows + Minus (-) to zoom out
Press Windows + Escape to close the Magnifier
result: The screen magnifies in real time as you move your mouse. Use it to read small text, inspect images, or for accessibility.

title: Google Reverse Image Search from Right-Click
description: Find where any image came from, identify people, landmarks, or products — directly from your browser.
steps: Right-click any image on any webpage
Select 'Search image with Google' (Chrome) or 'Search the web for this image' (Edge)
Google shows visually similar images and pages where the image appears
result: Identify fake news photos, find the original source, buy products by photo, or find higher-resolution versions of any image.

title: Markdown in WhatsApp and Telegram
description: Format your messages with bold, italic, strikethrough, and monospace — without any settings to toggle.
steps: Bold: *your text* (wrap with asterisks)
Italic: _your text_ (wrap with underscores)
Strikethrough: ~your text~ (wrap with tildes)
Monospace/code: `your text` (wrap with backticks)
In Telegram: **bold**, __italic__, `code`, ```code block```
result: Messages render with the formatting applied. Great for emphasis, corrections, or sharing code snippets in group chats.
